sources.title: Pods
sources.scope_notes: Backs pod fundamentals: pods as the smallest deployable unit, one-or-more container model, shared network namespace, shared storage, co-location, and the pod abstraction used by higher-level workload controllers.

sources.title: Pod Lifecycle
sources.scope_notes: Backs pod phases, restart behavior, termination, grace periods, container states, and troubleshooting language around Pending, Running, Succeeded, Failed, and CrashLoop-style failures.

sources.title: Liveness, Readiness, and Startup Probes
sources.scope_notes: Backs probe semantics, differences between liveness/readiness/startup probes, and how kubelet reacts to failing probes or holds readiness during startup.

sources.title: Init Containers
sources.scope_notes: Backs init-container ordering, run-to-completion behavior, differences from app containers, and pod startup sequencing before main containers begin.

sources.title: Deployments
sources.scope_notes: Backs Deployment behavior, Deployment-to-ReplicaSet-to-Pod ownership, rollout strategy, rolling updates, maxSurge/maxUnavailable behavior, rollout history, pause/resume, and rollback concepts.

sources.title: DaemonSet
sources.scope_notes: Backs one-pod-per-node semantics, automatic coverage of added nodes, common node-level use cases, selective node placement via nodeSelector/affinity, and DaemonSet toleration behavior.

sources.title: StatefulSets
sources.scope_notes: Backs stable pod identity, ordinal naming, stable storage via volumeClaimTemplates, ordered deployment and rolling updates, headless Service requirements, and DNS/network identity behavior.

sources.title: Jobs
sources.scope_notes: Backs run-to-completion semantics, backoffLimit, restartPolicy constraints, completions, parallelism, pod replacement on failure, and batch workload behavior distinct from Deployments.

sources.title: CronJob
sources.scope_notes: Backs scheduled Job execution, cron syntax expectations, concurrencyPolicy, history limits, suspend behavior, and controller behavior for missed schedules and overlapping runs.

sources.title: Resource Management for Pods and Containers
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for CPU and memory requests and limits, resource units, scheduler use of requests, allocatable-fit behavior, OOMKilled examples, and in-place Pod resize as stable in Kubernetes 1.35.

sources.title: Limit Ranges
sources.scope_notes: Supports claims about namespace-scoped minimum and maximum resource constraints, default requests and limits, and admission-time enforcement via LimitRange.

sources.title: Resource Quotas
sources.scope_notes: Supports claims about namespace resource governance, aggregate consumption limits, object-count quotas, and the relationship between quotas and resource requests/limits.

sources.title: Assigning Pods to Nodes
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for nodeSelector, node affinity, inter-pod affinity and anti-affinity, built-in topology labels, and the core node-placement model used by the scheduler.

sources.title: Taints and Tolerations
sources.scope_notes: Supports claims about NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, NoExecute behavior, taint-based repulsion, node-condition taints, and toleration-driven scheduling and eviction interactions.

sources.title: ConfigMaps
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for non-sensitive configuration, env-var and file-based injection, immutability, key/value structure, size limits, and update propagation behavior for mounted ConfigMaps.

sources.title: Secrets
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for Secret semantics, security caveats, env-var and volume consumption, optional and missing-key behavior, immutability, update propagation, and subPath update caveats.

sources.title: Pod Priority and Preemption
sources.scope_notes: Supports claims about PriorityClass, scheduling order, preemption flow, nominatedNodeName, graceful termination of preemption victims, and best-effort interaction with PodDisruptionBudgets.

sources.title: Pod Quality of Service Classes
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for Guaranteed, Burstable, and BestEffort classification criteria and for high-level eviction ordering under node resource pressure.

sources.title: Pod Lifecycle
sources.scope_notes: Supports claims about Pod phases, scheduling/binding terminology, graceful termination, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, preStop hook execution during shutdown, and Pod resize status conditions in v1.35.

sources.title: Horizontal Pod Autoscaling
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for HPA behavior in Kubernetes 1.35: purpose, control-loop cadence, scaling formula, min/max bounds, multiple metrics, custom/external metrics support, stabilization behavior, and autoscaling/v2 examples.

sources.title: kubectl autoscale
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for imperative HPA creation syntax and CLI semantics in Kubernetes 1.35, including current flags such as --min, --max, --cpu, and --memory.

sources.title: Kubernetes Metrics Server
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for metrics-server installation manifest URL, requirements, role in the built-in autoscaling pipeline, kubectl top support, 15-second collection interval, and common local-cluster TLS caveats.

sources.title: Tools for Monitoring Resources
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for the resource metrics pipeline and for explaining how HPA/VPA consume metrics.k8s.io, and how custom/external metrics require a fuller metrics pipeline with adapters.

sources.title: Prometheus Adapter for Kubernetes Metrics APIs
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for a concrete custom.metrics.k8s.io and external.metrics.k8s.io adapter example that can back claims about custom-metric-based HPA setups.

sources.title: Vertical Pod Autoscaling
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for comparing HPA and VPA, including VPA purpose, components, dependency on metrics, and update modes such as InPlaceOrRecreate.

sources.title: Resize CPU and Memory Resources assigned to Containers
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for Kubernetes 1.35 in-place container resource resize capability, useful when verifying claims that newer VPA modes can leverage in-place resize instead of always recreating Pods.

sources.title: Node Autoscaling
sources.scope_notes: Primary source for comparing workload autoscaling with node autoscaling and for explaining how Cluster Autoscaler-style node provisioning complements HPA-created Pods.
